headline: France records its hottest day with relief on the horizon
description: With an average day-night temperature of 30°C, June 24 set a new heat record in France. Forecasters expect a gradual end to the heatwave in the western part of the country by the end of the week. High temperatures will persist until next Tuesday or Wednesday in the rest of the country.

headline: New York Mayor Mamdani cements his hold on power after three of his candidates win Democratic primaries
Two strong earthquakes strike Venezuela, buildings collapse in Caracas
Ukrainian strikes on Russian refineries are triggering shortages and rising discontent
description: For the past three months, Kyiv's drones have targeted Russian refineries, ports, depots and pipelines, causing ripple effects throughout the Russian economy. Gasoline has become scarce at service stations.

headline: Two killed in Israeli strike in South Lebanon, Hezbollah denounces true 'violation'
description: Two people were killed in southern Lebanon on Wednesday in an Israeli drone strike that Hezbollah said was the latest breach of a ceasefire agreement between the two sides.

headline: China remains on the sidelines of the World Cup
description: The global superpower, which relishes the opportunity to excel at major international events, has only managed to qualify once, in 2002. Despite Xi Jinping's determination, the poor performance of the national football team continues to disappoint Chinese fans.

headline: 250 years of American independence: A groundbreaking Constitution for 'a more perfect union'
description: 'America 250' (3/13). Eleven years after the founding of the United States, there was still much left to invent. The Constitution, adopted in September 1787, offered an unprecedented balance between the three branches of government in order to achieve 'a more perfect union' of the people. The first president, George Washington, elected in 1789, remains revered to this day.

Living under Paris's zinc roofs in 40°C heat: 'One day, the people living there will look for those responsible'
description: As France faces extreme heat, many of these top-story spaces have become uninhabitable. Some residents have formed groups to call on their landlords, neighbors and local authorities to address their living conditions.

headline: France faces unprecedented heatwave: 'We are about to experience four of the hottest days ever recorded in the country'
description: A prolonged heatwave, rather than a brief spike, has arrived. Unusually high temperatures are set to persist across the country until at least the end of this week, exposing more than 90% of the French population to extreme and rare heat.

headline: Serving a husband, God and America: Young female conservatives gather at San Antonio's Turning Point summit
description: In early June, around 3,000 women attended the Women's Leadership Summit in San Antonio, Texas. This annual event brings together a variety of conservative figures and influencers, relying on the extensive network of Turning Point USA, founded by right-wing activist Charlie Kirk, who was murdered in 2025. After convincing a majority of young men to vote for Trump in 2024, the organization now targets female voters from Gen Z.
